etymology.description: semantic 口 (mouth; speech) + phonetic 我 (sound: ó)
etymology.interpretation: The character 哦 (ó, 'oh') combines the semantic element 口 (kǒu, 'mouth') indicating speech or voice, with the phonetic component 我 (wǒ) indicating the sound. The presence of 口 suggests an exclamation or verbal expression, aligning with the meaning 'oh'.

examples.chinese: 哦，我知道了(哦，我知道了)。
哦，原来如此(哦，原來如此)。
examples.english: Oh, so that's how it is.

examples.chinese: 她突然说(她突然說)：“哦，我忘记了(哦，我忘記了)！”
examples.pinyin: Tā tūrán shuō: “Ó, wǒ wàngjì le!”
